title: Kustomize, Helm 4, Rendering, Validation, Field Ownership, and Promotion · Control-Plane Trace: K8s Kustomize Generators Hashes
summary: A platform team copies environment manifests, renders an unreviewed chart, stores credentials in values, and promotes mutable images without diff, ownership, or rollback evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s kustomize generators hashes increment in rendered environment release package. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Generate ConfigMaps and Secrets from external sources, reason about name hashes and references, restrict loading, and keep secret values out of rendered evidence.
Compose Kustomize resources, components, overlays, prefixes, namespaces, labels, images, replacements, and patches without corrupting selectors or source bases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example environment overlay drifts from base, Helm values change immutable fields, rendered resources contain a secret, and promotion rebuilds a new image. Render, diff, validate, pin, sign, promote the same artifact, and retain rollback evidence. Competency probe: Generate ConfigMaps and Secrets from external sources, reason about name hashes and references, restrict loading, and keep secret values out of rendered evidence. A defensible solution applies K8s Kustomize Generators Hashes, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to rendered environment release package.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-packaging-kustomize-helm-promotion-2
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-packaging-kustomize-helm-promotion-2
    artifact: release-package.yaml
    decision: worked k8s packaging kustomize helm promotion 2
    environment: rootless development cluster with an empty namespace
    fault-injection: make an admission dependency unavailable during an upgrade
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      base: reviewed-kubernetes-resources
      overlay: production-patches
      generators: hashed-config-no-secret-output
      helm: 4.2.2-oci-digest-pinned
      render: offline-and-server-validated
      field-manager: platform-reconciler
      promotion: immutable-digest
      rollback: compatibility-tested
    verify: kubectl kustomize overlays/production && helm template status oci://registry.example.test/status --version 1.4.0
    failure: Reject copied manifests, unpinned chart dependencies, secrets in values, render-only confidence, field ownership theft, or Git history as safe rollback proof.
    repair: Compose reviewed bases, render deterministically, validate schemas and policy, diff server behavior, preserve ownership, and promote immutable artifacts.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Disabling generator name hashes is always necessary to keep references stable. This fails the K8s Kustomize Generators Hashes contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
